title: Aegis Intelligence incident intake to standing proof
summary: An Aegis-style defense event remains detectable and replayable, but the attempted defensive consequence is denied because commit-time standing is stale.

review_time.incident_event: Anomalous Fog packets detected at the network gateway
review_time.proposed_consequence: rotate encryption keys and purge local cache

commit_time.requested_consequence: rotate encryption keys and purge local cache

standing_rule.description: A prior review does not carry execution standing unless authority, policy, evidence, and context remain current or are reauthorized at commit time.

receipt.reason: Detection and review survived, but consequence-binding standing was not current at commit time.

formalism_expectation.theorem: Detection does not imply authority; review does not imply standing; consequence requires commit-time standing.
